prompt: In SAM, what is the purpose of discovery model mapping for software assets?
choices: To link discovered software evidence to the correct normalized software model for reconciliation
To convert all software into hardware CIs
To assign every installation to the same license metric
To remove publisher names from discovered titles
explanation: Discovery model mapping bridges raw discovery data to normalized model records, enabling accurate entitlement and compliance logic.

prompt: A newly discovered title is repeatedly unmatched. Which approach should a SAM administrator take?
choices: Create a mapping rule or model mapping entry so future discoveries normalize automatically
Ignore unmatched titles unless they appear in incident records
Delete the discovered software records weekly
Disable normalization jobs for that publisher
explanation: Persistent unmatched titles indicate missing mapping logic and should be addressed to improve normalization completeness.

prompt: Why is validation with publisher and version details important when creating discovery model mappings?
choices: It ensures the mapped model represents the correct licensable product variant
It increases incident SLA accuracy
It prevents contract renewals from being created
It bypasses reconciliation exceptions automatically
explanation: Accurate publisher/version alignment prevents mapping errors that can distort entitlement consumption and compliance results.

prompt: What is the main risk of overly broad discovery mapping rules?
choices: Too many CMDB relationship types
Incorrectly grouping distinct products under one software model, causing compliance inaccuracies
Slower catalog item approvals
Missing knowledge base categories
explanation: Broad rules can misclassify products and produce misleading reconciliation outcomes, especially for publishers with complex portfolios.

prompt: Which KPI best reflects improvement in discovery model quality over time?
choices: Count of ITSM incidents linked to software
Decrease in unmatched discovered software and fewer remaps after reconciliation review
Number of dashboard widgets created
Total users in the SAM admin group
explanation: Lower unmatched volume and fewer correction cycles indicate healthier mapping logic and better normalization quality.

prompt: What best describes the relationship between Application Portfolio Management (APM) and Strategic Portfolio Management (SPM)?
choices: APM and SPM are unrelated products with no shared data model
APM focuses on application inventory/rationalization insights that feed broader SPM investment and strategy decisions
SPM is only used for infrastructure discovery
APM replaces all strategic planning workflows in SPM
explanation: APM provides application-centric portfolio intelligence, while SPM governs strategic planning, prioritization, and execution across portfolios.

prompt: A leadership team wants to reduce redundant applications while funding strategic priorities. Which capability pairing is most appropriate?
choices: Use APM rationalization insights and execute investment decisions through SPM planning and governance
Use SPM only and ignore application lifecycle data
Use APM only and skip strategic objective mapping
Use ITSM change requests as the sole investment mechanism
explanation: APM identifies rationalization opportunities; SPM converts those insights into governed investment and execution plans.

prompt: Which statement is accurate for CIS exam context when comparing APM and SPM?
choices: APM is primarily about strategic objective trees, while SPM tracks only software lifecycle dates
SPM is strictly a project execution tool with no strategic planning
APM informs what to invest in at the application level; SPM governs how strategic investments are selected and delivered
Both modules provide identical capabilities and should be implemented interchangeably
explanation: The distinction is scope: APM provides application portfolio decision support, and SPM handles enterprise strategy-to-execution governance.

prompt: What is a practical governance benefit of integrating APM data into SPM planning?
choices: It automatically closes all technical debt items
It removes the need for portfolio review boards
It enables investment decisions to consider business value, risk, cost, and application health together
It limits visibility to only one business unit
explanation: Integrated data improves governance quality by adding application health and risk context to strategic funding decisions.

prompt: If an organization tracks app technical fit in APM but does not connect it to SPM priorities, what is the likely outcome?
choices: Better strategy execution with less data
Infrastructure incidents decrease automatically
Portfolio decisions may miss modernization urgency and strategic alignment opportunities
APM data becomes mandatory for ITSM incident closure
explanation: Without strategic linkage, APM insights are less likely to influence funding and roadmap decisions where they create business value.
